title: The New Definition of Career Success
subtitle: 从终身雇佣到自由职业

paragraphs.en: Success was once defined as being able to stay at a company for a long time and move up the corporate ladder. The goal was to reach the top, accumulate wealth and retire to a life of ease. My father is a successful senior executive.
paragraphs.zh: 成功曾被定义为能够在一家公司长期工作并沿着企业阶梯不断晋升。目标是到达顶端、积累财富，然后退休过上安逸的生活。我的父亲就是一位成功的高管。

paragraphs.en: In 35 years, he worked for only three companies. When I started my career, things were already different. If you weren't changing companies every three or four years, you simply weren't getting ahead in your career.
paragraphs.zh: 35年间，他只为三家公司工作过。当我开始职业生涯时，情况已经不同了。如果你不是每三四年换一次公司，那你的职业发展基本上就停滞不前了。

paragraphs.en: But back then, if you were a consultant or freelancer, people would wonder what was wrong with you. They would assume you had problems getting a job. Today, consulting or freelancing for five businesses at the same time is a badge of honor.
paragraphs.zh: 但在那时，如果你是顾问或自由职业者，人们会疑惑你是不是出了什么问题。他们会以为你找不到正式工作。而如今，同时为五家企业提供咨询或做自由职业是一种荣誉的象征。

paragraphs.en: It shows how valuable an individual is. Many companies now look to these "ultimate professionals" to solve problems their full-time teams can't. Or they save money by hiring "top-tier experts" only for particular projects.
paragraphs.zh: 这体现了一个人的价值所在。如今许多公司都会寻找这些“终极专业人士”来解决全职团队无法解决的问题。或者通过仅为特定项目聘用“顶尖专家”来节约成本。

words.meaning: adj. 顶级的，最高层次的

paragraphs.en: Working at home or in cafes, starting businesses of their own, and even launching business ventures that eventually may fail, all indicate "initiative," "creativity," and "adaptability," which are desirable qualities in today's workplace. Most important, there is a growing recognition that people who balance multiple roles and responsibilities tend to be better problem-solvers and more innovative thinkers.
paragraphs.zh: 在家或咖啡馆工作、创办自己的企业，甚至启动可能最终失败的商业项目，这些都体现了“主动性”、“创造力”和“适应能力”——这些都是当今职场中备受青睐的品质。最重要的是，越来越多的人认识到，能够平衡多重角色和职责的人往往是更优秀的问题解决者和更具创新思维的人。
